trajectory.description: Observation: Starting first iteration with no prior baseline. Analysis: Need to establish working pipeline with pretrained wav2vec2-base + simple mean-pool classifier for bonafide/spoof logits before optimizing. Decision: Implement model/train/predict scripts for 2 epochs on full train+dev (to get EER), test predict.py on small dev subset to validate contract, then submit_eval for official test score and time measurement.

trajectory.command: ls -la; echo '=== TRAIN.PY ==='; cat train.py; echo '=== MODEL.PY ==='; cat model.py; echo '=== PREDICT.PY ==='; cat predict.py
trajectory.command_output_summary:
h.no_grad():
        for input_values, _, attention_mask in loader:
            input_values = input_values.to(device)
            attention_mask = attention_mask.to(device)
            logits = model(input_values, attention_mask)
            predictions.extend(logits.cpu().tolist())
    result = {'predictions': predictions}
    with open(args.output, 'w') as f:
        json.dump(result, f)
    print(f'Saved {len(predictions)} predictions to {args.output}')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
trajectory.description: Observation: Training failed at first batch in collate_fn (line 47 of train.py) with ValueError on inhomogeneous shapes (batch of 16 waveforms) when calling feature_extractor. This occurs because list of torch.Tensors of varying lengths cannot be directly np.array'ed internally. History shows initial pipeline setup with wav2vec2-base + classifier for 2 epochs. Analysis: Root cause is mismatch in data type for variable-length audio input to Wav2Vec2FeatureExtractor (expects list of np.ndarray). No eval submitted yet. Decision: Inspect full current code in all files, then rewrite train.py with fixed collate_fn (convert to .numpy()), proper DataLoader setup using protocol parsing, binary labels (bonafide=1), BCEWithLogitsLoss, and logging to metrics.jsonl. Keep model.py and predict.py if functional or update minimally for consistency. Submit eval after short training to get baseline EER and measure time.

No training/eval submitted yet after 3 iters; last command only inspected predict.py. Analysis: Root cause confirmed in collate (waveforms from torchaudio are tensors of diff lengths); fix is explicit .numpy() conversion to list of ndarray. Batch prints violate stdout rules. Decision: Edit train.py (fix collate, remove per-batch prints, bump train batch to 32) + predict.py (fix collate, bump batch to 64), run 2-epoch training for baseline, then immediately submit_eval on best.pt to obtain first test EER and measure per-iter time.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training crashes in DataLoader worker with AttributeError on inputs.attention_mask because the BatchFeature from feature_extractor sometimes lacks 'attention_mask' key (likely when no padding occurs or default config). This matches the history of collate_fn issues. No EER score yet after 4 iterations. Analysis: The root cause is inconsistent return of attention_mask from Wav2Vec2FeatureExtractor(..., padding=True). Forcing return_attention_mask=True in the call ensures it's always present. Additionally, stdout prints of metrics violate guidelines (use metrics.jsonl only), and we can increase batch sizes for better GPU utilization. Decision: Update collate_fn in both train.py and predict.py to include return_attention_mask=True. Remove epoch print statements from train.py, increase batch sizes (train=64, dev=128, predict=128), run 3-epoch training to get a baseline, then submit_eval on the checkpoint to obtain first EER measurement and time benchmark. This follows the 'first iteration with few epochs + submit_eval' guideline despite being iter 5.

trajectory.description: Observation: OOM error during backward pass with batch_train=64/dev=128 on wav2vec2-base (26.9GiB used, trying to alloc ~5GiB more). Previous collate/attention_mask issues fixed but no EER yet after 5 iters. Analysis: Variable-length padding + full fine-tune in fp32 + large dev eval batches cause peak mem during grad. Root cause confirmed by error trace. Decision: Add os.environ for expandable_segments, reduce batches (train=16, dev=32), introduce GradScaler+autocast(fp16) in train/eval loops for ~50% mem reduction, increase num_workers=8 for throughput, update predict.py similarly with autocast and batch=64. Run 3-epoch train then submit_eval for first baseline EER and time measurement per workflow rules.

Model loading showed unexpected keys but continued; no EER obtained after 5 prior failed iters. Analysis: Root cause is incompatible autocast API usage (also present in predict.py); switching to autocast(dtype=torch.bfloat16), removing GradScaler (unneeded for bf16), adding best-model saving based on dev EER, increasing batches (64/256) for GPU utilization per guidance, using tolist() for scores, and bumping to 3 epochs follows 'few epochs + submit_eval' rule while fixing all prior collate/AMP/OOM issues. Decision: Update train.py and predict.py with these minimal targeted fixes, run training to produce best.pt baseline, then submit_eval immediately to get first official EER and iteration time benchmark.

trajectory.description: Observation: Still OOM (group_norm in wav2vec2 encoder) on bs=64/256 after 163s run; no EER yet after 6 failed iters. History shows repeated collate/AMP/OOM issues now resolved except memory. Analysis: Root cause is large padded batches on variable-length audio (up to 15s) causing activation spikes in conv/feature layers during full fine-tune+eval; bf16+expandable_segments insufficient without length cap. Decision: Add max_length=160000+truncation=True to both collates (caps mem, most utts <5s), lower batches (train=16/dev=64/predict=128), fix autocast import+context to torch.amp('cuda', bfloat16) in train+predict for compatibility, keep 5 epochs+best-EER-save+full fine-tune (lr=1e-5). This gets working baseline per 'few epochs + immediate submit_eval' rule while measuring time; edit existing files minimally.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline EER=0.1181 (acc@EER=0.8853) achieved with wav2vec2-base after 5 epochs; loss drops rapidly to ~0.004 indicating quick fit on seen attacks but room for better OOD generalization on codecs/unseen attacks (test EER still >10%). Metrics show dev EER logged per epoch; model loading has expected 'unexpected keys' for quantizer (normal for Wav2Vec2Model). No OOM this run thanks to max_length+bf16. Analysis: Current SpoofDetector (not yet inspected) is likely basic mean-pool+linear; literature shows WavLM + stats pooling (mean+std) + small MLP head significantly lowers EER on ASVspoof LA. Larger backbone (wavlm-large 317M <1B limit) needs batch reduction. Time left (7.6h) and ~18min/iter allows ~10 epochs safely. Decision: First inspect current model.py + metrics (per critical rule before any edit), then in next iter update to AutoModel+stats-pool+MLP with default='microsoft/wavlm-large', smaller batches, more epochs, and submit_eval for improved score.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline EER=0.1181 (acc@EER=0.8853) with wav2vec2-base after 5 epochs; loss drops to ~0.004 by epoch 1 (rapid convergence on seen attacks). Model print confirms Wav2Vec2Model + mean-pool + Linear(768->1) head (94M params). Metrics show dev_eer improving then plateauing. Analysis: Simple head + smaller backbone limits OOD generalization to unseen attacks (A07+) and telephony codecs; literature (ASVspoof) favors WavLM-large + stats pooling (mean+std) + MLP with dropout/BN for lower EER. No OOM thanks to max_length=160k + bf16. Time (~18min/iter from history) and 7.6h budget allow ~10-15 more iters with larger model. Decision: Cat current model.py + metrics.jsonl (per critical 'always check workspace before edit' rule) to enable precise incremental edits next; will then switch pretrained='microsoft/wavlm-large', update to StatsPooling+MLP head, lower bs (train=8/dev=32/predict=64), raise epochs=10, keep best-EER checkpoint logic.

Observation: Baseline test EER=0.1181 (dev EER~0.0004) with wav2vec2-base + mean-pool + linear head after 5 epochs; rapid loss drop to ~0.003 indicates heavy overfitting to seen attacks (A01-A06). Model print confirms 94M params, simple architecture. WavLM-large is pre-cached. Analysis: Large generalization gap due to weak backbone/head for OOD test (unseen attacks A07+, telephony codecs, disjoint speakers). Stats pooling (mean+std) + MLP with BN/dropout + WavLM-large (317M) is standard for ASVspoof LA and stays under 1B params. History shows max_length+bf16+small bs prevents OOM. Decision: Rewrite model.py with WavLMModel+StatsPool+MLP; update train.py (pretrained='microsoft/wavlm-large', bs_train=8/bs_dev=32/epochs=8, add weight_decay) and predict.py (same pretrained, bs=64) for compatibility. Run training to save best.pt on dev EER, then submit_eval immediately per workflow for new baseline and time measurement (~expected 40min).

trajectory.description: Observation: Training failed immediately with TypeError: BCEWithLogitsLoss.forward() takes 3 positional arguments but 4 were given due to malformed loss call with ternary and extra arg after model forward succeeded. Best test EER remains 0.1181 (dev EER ~0.0004 after 5 epochs on wav2vec2-base + simple mean-pool + linear). Rapid loss drop to ~0.003 indicates severe overfitting to seen attacks A01-A06. Analysis: Loss bug is from fragile dim-handling code (old model returned shape that triggered conditional); root cause of poor test EER is insufficient backbone/head for OOD generalization (unseen A07-A19 attacks, telephony codecs, disjoint speakers). WavLM-large (317M params) + stats pooling (mean+std) + BN/ReLU/dropout MLP is standard for ASVspoof LA and fits <1B limit. Prior max_length=160k + bf16 + small bs prevented OOM. History confirms model.py is still old (768-dim linear head). Decision: Rewrite model.py (new architecture with get_pretrain_name), minimally edit train.py (fix loss to criterion(outputs, labels), ensure squeezed logits), run 8-epoch training then submit_eval for strong new baseline and updated time measurement.

trajectory.description: Observation: WavLM-large + stats-pool + MLP achieved test EER=0.094 (from 0.118 baseline), with dev EER~0 after 8 epochs and rapid loss drop to ~0.003. Analysis: Severe overfitting to seen attacks (A01-A06) in train/dev; insufficient robustness to OOD test (unseen A07-A19, telephony codecs, disjoint speakers). Current aug absent, regularization moderate (dropout=0.1, wd=1e-4), no scheduler. WavLM cache loads successfully. Decision: Add simple train-time aug (random gain + low noise) for robustness, raise classifier dropout to 0.3, wd=1e-3, add CosineAnnealingLR, set 12 epochs + keep safe bs=8/32. Update model.py (dropout), train.py (aug, AutoFeatureExtractor, scheduler, params), predict.py (AutoFeatureExtractor). Run full training then submit_eval on best.pt for improved OOD EER and time check.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best EER improved to 0.0858 (from 0.094) using WavLM-large + stats-pool + MLP (dropout=0.3), mild waveform aug (gain ~[-3,3]dB + low noise p=0.3), wd=1e-3, cosine scheduler over 12 epochs; dev EER reaches ~0.0 rapidly with train loss ~0.003 (metrics confirm quick convergence). Analysis: Severe overfitting to seen attacks A01-A06; insufficient robustness to test distribution shift (unseen A07-A19, telephony codecs, new speakers). Stronger aug (wider gain, random SNR noise), higher dropout=0.5, increased wd=5e-3, lower lr=5e-6 + longer 15-epoch schedule should improve OOD generalization without changing architecture. predict.py and saving logic already functional. Decision: Incrementally update model.py (dropout), train.py (aug strength, hypers, scheduler T_max=15), keep predict.py; run full training then submit_eval to get next EER and stay within time/iter budget (~2h per run, 3.9h left).
